subclassFluff.name: Circle of the Titan
entries: {@i Wreak Colossal Havoc}

subclassFluff.name: Demonic Sorcery
entries: {@i Summon the Powers of the Abyss}

subclassFluff.name: Hell Knight
entries: {@i Inflict Hellish Wounds and Damn Enemies}

subclassFluff.name: Pestilence Domain
entries: {@i Foment Plague and Rot}

feat.name: Arcane Restoration
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Essence Rejuvenation
entries: When you use Soul Siphon to consume a soul, you can choose one or more expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level equal to no more than 4. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Death Knight Ascension
entries: Your path to becoming a death knight is complete. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Undead
entries: Your creature type is Undead.

entries.name: Unholy Anatomy
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic and Poison damage. You don't gain {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels from dehydration, malnutrition, or suffocation.

entries.name: Hellfire Orb
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 to 5 Death Points to throw an orb of pure hellfire at a point you can see within 120 feet. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on the chosen point makes a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Charisma modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d6} Fire damage and {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage per Death Point expended. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage.

feat.name: Death Knight Initiate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Death Points
entries: Your dedication to the path of the Death Knight gives you access to profane powers of undeath. Your ability to use such powers is represented by Death Points. You have a number of Death Points equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You regain all expended Death Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You can expend Death Points to use certain Path of the Death Knight benefits. This feat gives you one such benefit: Dread Strike.

entries.name: Dread Strike
entries: You always have the {@spell Wrathful Smite|XPHB} spell prepared. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. You can cast it without expending a spell slot by expending 1 Death Point. When you expend Death Points to cast {@spell Wrathful Smite|XPHB}, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws to avoid or end the spell's effect. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

feat.name: Deathly Presence
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Awful Presence
entries: You always have the {@spell Fear|XPHB} spell prepared. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. You can cast it without expending a spell slot by expending 1 Death Point. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. When you expend Death Points to cast {@spell Fear|XPHB}, you deal 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Psychic damage to each creature that fails its saving throw against the spell, in addition to the spell's normal effects.

feat.name: Dread Authority
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dread Command
entries: You always have the {@spell Command|XPHB} spell prepared. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. You can cast it without expending a spell slot by expending 1 Death Point. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. When you expend Death Points to cast {@spell Command|XPHB}, Undead targeted by it have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw against the spell.

feat.name: Harbinger of Doom
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Ill Omen
entries: You always have the {@spell Bane|XPHB} spell prepared. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. You can cast it without expending a spell slot by expending 1 Death Point. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have. When you expend Death Points to cast {@spell Bane|XPHB}, affected targets subtract {@dice 1d6} from attack rolls and saving throws instead of {@dice 1d4}.

feat.name: Lich Ascension
entries: Your path to lichdom is complete. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Undead
entries: Your creature type is Undead.

entries.name: Unholy Anatomy
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic and Poison damage. You don't gain {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels from dehydration, malnutrition, or suffocation.

entries.name: Frightening Gaze
entries: You learn the {@spell Fear|XPHB} spell if you don't already know it, and you always have it prepared. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can cast the spell without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Rejuvenation
entries: If you die, you re-form in {@dice 1d10} days if you have a spirit jar and aren't revived before then. You gain a new body, appearing with all your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of your spirit jar.

feat.name: Lich Initiate
entries: You take the first steps toward lichdom, which involve creating your spirit jar, a magical vessel that anchors your soul to the world of the living in the event of your body's destruction.
You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Creating Your Spirit Jar
entries: Choose a Tiny object of great significance to you. Roll on or choose from the {@table Spirit Jars|XUA2026VillainousOptions} table to inspire this object. You spend a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} anchoring your soul to this object, allowing you to consume the souls of the living to bolster your own power. You can have only one spirit jar at a time. If you create a second spirit jar, the old one is destroyed.

entries.name: Spirit Jar Destruction
entries: Your spirit jar's {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equals {@dcYourSpellSave}, and it has a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your spellcasting ability modifier plus your character level. If your spirit jar is destroyed, you gain 2 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels, and you can't use the Soul Siphon ability until you create a new one.

entries.name: Soul Siphon
entries: When you reduce a Humanoid enemy to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can consume its soul and gain a boost of arcane energy (no action required). On your next turn, the first creature you hit with an attack takes extra Necrotic damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your spellcasting ability modifier. You also gain this benefit if someone else reduces a Humanoid enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. A soul consumed in this way can be restored only by a {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} or {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.

feat.name: Transfer Life
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Soul Transference
entries: When you use Soul Siphon to consume a soul, you can choose a creature within 60 feet of yourself to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Point}).

feat.name: Undead Grasp
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Paralyzing Touch
entries: You know the {@spell Chill Touch|XPHB} cantrip. If you already know it, you learn another cantrip of your choice. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

When you deal damage with {@spell Chill Touch|XPHB}, you can expend a level 1+ spell slot to attempt to paralyze the target. The target takes an extra {@damage 1d10} Necrotic damage per level of the spell slot expended and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against {@dcYourSpellSave} or have the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

feat.name: Unholy Steed
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Spectral Steed
entries: You always have the {@spell Find Steed|XPHB} spell prepared. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell. You can cast it without expending a spell slot by expending 1 Death Point. When you expend Death Points to cast {@spell Find Steed|XPHB}, the summoned steed is a Fiend, and targets you choose have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the Wisdom saving throw against its Fell Glare. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

charoption.name: Path of the Death Knight
entries: Death knights are dastards, deserters, and disgraced champions, undead warriors who march toward perdition to fulfill selfish desires and slake their bloodthirst. A death knight's martial prowess is inextricably tied to their evil nature, but their heinous deeds extend far beyond the battlefield.

entries.name: Death Knight Journeys
entries: While each death knight is unique, every death knight's descent into villainy begins with a significant and dishonorable act—a deliberate wrongdoing or shameful mistake that marks their disgrace. You can roll on or choose from the {@table Death Knight Journeys|XUA2026VillainousOptions} table to determine why your character has chosen to walk this path.

entries.name: Death Knights Across yhe Multiverse
entries: The death knights of D&D are dramatic figures whose tragic fates result from equally tragic sins. On many worlds, death knights' misdeeds spell doom not just for themselves and their companions but for entire kingdoms.
Notable death knights across the multiverse include the following:

items.name: {@creature Lord Soth|DSotDQ},
entries: Knight of the Black Rose and ruler of Dargaard Keep on the world of Krynn

items.name: Olanthius,
entries: former general of the angel Zariel who became a death knight after Zariel's fall from grace

items.name: Saint Kargoth,
entries: favored servant of the demon lord Demogorgon and Greyhawk's first death knight

While most death knights refuse to atone for the acts that made them, it's possible for a death knight to repent and end their curse. Such was the fate of Lord Vanrak Moonstar of Waterdeep, who repented to {@deity Selûne|Forgotten Realms|PHB} for betraying her in favor of her nemesis, {@deity Shar|Forgotten Realms|PHB}.
For more on {@creature death knight|XMM|death knights}, see the {@book Monster Manual|XMM}.

entries.name: Path of the Death Knight Feats
entries: The following feats represent one path a character can take to become a death knight. To complete the Path of the Death Knight, begin by taking the {@feat Death Knight Initiate|XUA2026VillainousOptions} feat, followed by any other Path of the Death Knight feat you choose. Finally, when you reach level 12 or higher, take the {@feat Death Knight Ascension|XUA2026VillainousOptions} feat.

charoption.name: Path of the Lich
entries: Liches are powerful spellcasters who have conquered death through the relentless pursuit of arcane power. All liches undergo a ritual that binds their soul to a spirit jar, a personal and well-protected object that allows the lich's destroyed body to re-form as long as the object is intact.

entries.name: Lich Rites
entries: The path to lichdom is unique to each who walks it. To complete the transformation, aspirants must fulfill a personal and often profane ritual. The {@table Lich Rites|XUA2026VillainousOptions} table contains examples of such rituals to inspire your journey.

entries.name: Liches Across the Multiverse
entries: While the most infamous liches in D&D represent evil incarnate, liches can vary in alignments, motivations, and deeds. Those who achieve lichdom make their mark on the multiverse as demigods and despots, mentors and protectors, masters and manipulators. These storied beings include the following:

items.name: {@creature Renwick|PotA|Renwick Caradoon},
entries: founder of the Knights of Samular in the city of Waterdeep

items.name: Szass Tam,
entries: Thay's zulkir of necromancy

items.name: {@creature Vecna the Archlich|VEoR},
entries: Oerthian king and demigod of secrets and evil magic

items.name: Vlaakith the Lich-Queen,
entries: the iron-fisted leader of the githyanki

items.name: {@creature Zlan|FRAiF},
entries: the frozen amalgamation of the seven liches who created the {@i Crystal Shard}
All liches have in common their arcane prowess, their undead nature, and the sequestering of their souls. For more on {@creature lich|XMM|liches}, see the {@book Monster Manual|XMM}.

entries.name: Path of the Lich Feats
entries: The following Path of the Lich feats represent one path a character can take to become a lich. To complete the Path of the Lich, begin by taking the {@feat Lich Initiate|XUA2026VillainousOptions} feat, followed by any other Path of the Lich feat you choose. Finally, when you reach level 12 or higher, take the {@feat Lich Ascension|XUA2026VillainousOptions} feat.

table.name: Death Knight Journeys
colLabels: You Fell from Grace When You...
rows: Made a pact with a malevolent entity such as a demon lord.

table.name: Lich Rites
colLabels: To Conduct Your Ritual, You Must...
rows: Consume one hundred souls to strengthen your magic.
Decipher and recite a forbidden incantation from a lost tome written in a dead language.
Use ancient abjuration magic to conceal yourself from the gods of death.
Pen a blasphemous verse and perform it in a defiled temple.
Craft a potion from your victims' remains and imbibe it beneath a full moon.
Corrupt your soul so completely no afterlife will accept it.

table.name: Spirit Jars
rows: An acorn from a forest destroyed long ago.
A love letter from a deceased paramour.
The inkwell that you used to scribe your first spell or prayer.
The defaced holy symbol of a god you've denounced.
A bell whose toll becomes lower with each soul you collect.
A desiccated body part—such as an eye, finger, or horn—that was once part of you or someone you knew.

data.name: Unearthed Arcana 2026: Villainous Options
entries: This playtest document is part of a series of {@i Unearthed Arcana} articles that present material designed for upcoming products. The material here uses the rules in the {@book 2024 Player's Handbook|XPHB}.

entries.name: What's Inside
entries: This document presents four new subclasses:

entries.name: Content Warning
entries: This material contains descriptions of body horror, disease, and insects that some readers might find disturbing.

entries.name: This Is Playtest Material
entries: This article is presented for playtesting and feedback. The options here are experimental and in draft form. They aren't officially part of the game. Your feedback will help determine whether we adopt it as official.

items.name: How to Playtest This UA
entries: We invite you to try out this material in play. To play with this material, you may either incorporate it into your campaign or run one or more special playtest sessions. For such a session, you may create an adventure of your own or use a short adventure from a source like {@adventure Dragon Delves|DrDe-BD}.

items.name: Power Level
entries: The character options you read here might be more or less powerful than options in the {@book 2024 Player's Handbook|XPHB}. If a design survives playtesting, we adjust its power to the desirable level before publication. This means an option could be more or less powerful in its final form.

items.name: Feedback
entries: The best way for you to give us feedback on this material is in the survey we'll release on D&D Beyond. If we make this material official, it will be refined based on your feedback, and then it will appear in a D&D book.
Providing feedback on this document is one way you can help shape the future of D&D!

entries.name: Subclasses
entries: This section presents the following subclasses: {@class Cleric|XPHB|Pestilence Domain|Pestilence|XUA2026VillainousOptions}, {@class Druid|XPHB|Circle of the Titan|Titan|XUA2026VillainousOptions}, {@class Fighter|XPHB|Hell Knight|Hell Knight|XUA2026VillainousOptions}, and {@class Sorcerer|XPHB|Demonic Sorcery|Demonic|XUA2026VillainousOptions}.

entries.name: Pestilence Domain (Cleric)
entries: {@i Foment Plague and Rot}

entries.name: Circle of the Titan (Druid)
entries: {@i Wreak Colossal Havoc}

entries.name: Hell Knight (Fighter)
entries: {@i Inflict Hellish Wounds and Damn Enemies}

entries.name: Demonic Sorcery (Sorcerer)
entries: {@i Summon the Powers of the Abyss}

entries.name: Paths of Villainy
entries: This section includes two paths: the {@charoption Path of the Death Knight|XUA2026VillainousOptions} and the {@charoption Path of the Lich|XUA2026VillainousOptions}. Each path presents a selection of feats that represent a character's transformation into one of these two villainous archetypes.
